_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine national autopilot)
_meta.note: VA-02 (Virginia 2nd Congressional District) cluster — federal tier. Incumbent Rep. Jen Kiggans (R) + the 2026 Democratic challenger field. Evidence-first finalization. TWO HIGH-VALUE CURRENCY CATCHES: John 'Burk' Stringfellow and Nicolaus Sleister did NOT make the May 27 2026 DPVA-certified Aug 4 primary ballot (certified VA-02 D list = Devanath, Bill Fleming, Luria, Mosolf per Blue Virginia 2026-05-27) — both set status:lost / candidacy_status:not_running. Luria (former VA-02 Rep 2019-2023) scored on her real congressional roll-call record; she remains the certified primary frontrunner. Devanath certified on the ballot and scored from her 19-point campaign issues page. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS flagged: Kiggans (R) co-sponsored an IVF-coverage mandate (Bipartisan IVF for Military Families Act) and the DIGNIDAD/Dignity Act legalization pathway (~12M) — both scored FALSE with citations against the conservative line. Luria (D) scored two FALSEs on foreign_policy_restraint reflecting documented hawkishness (only Democrat to vote against repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF; voted against the Iran War Powers Resolution). Devanath (D) earned two TRUEs on foreign_policy_restraint from the anti-war left (end endless wars; rejects AIPAC money) — credit where earned. NULL where no public record (no party inference).

profile.background: U.S. Representative, VA-02 (Virginia Beach/Norfolk); elected 2022, serving in the 119th Congress and seeking reelection 2026. Navy helicopter pilot veteran; geriatric nurse practitioner; former Virginia state senator (SD-7). House Armed Services Committee. A moderate, swing-district Republican (Cook PVI near even). Heritage Action 119th ~74% session / 66% lifetime.

jen-kiggans.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-02 (Virginia Beach/Norfolk); Navy pilot, nurse practitioner, ex-VA state senator; HASC member; 2026 reelection. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Solidly conservative on life (with exceptions), borders, elections, women's sports, and defense, but with DOCUMENTED LEFT-BREAKS scored FALSE: (1) co-introduced the standalone Bipartisan IVF for Military Families Act and championed the IVF-coverage provision through HASC/House — an IVF-coverage mandate the embryo-personhood position opposes; (2) named GOP cosponsor of the DIGNIDAD/Dignity Act (H.R.4393), a renewable legal-status pathway for ~12M undocumented since before 12/31/2020 — contrary to mandatory deportation. Conservative-direction HARD votes scored TRUE: Protecting Health Care for All Patients Act (H.R.485, bans QALY/quality-of-life rationing); Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (trans-athlete ban); Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5); SAVE Act (documentary proof-of-citizenship to register); H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (border wall + national E-Verify mandate); hawkish on foreign policy (FOR the Apr-2024 Ukraine/Israel/Taiwan supplementals, criticized GOP 'isolationists') scored FALSE on foreign_policy_restraint. FLAGGED-BUT-UNSCORED soft deviations (left null pending firmer sourcing): as a VA state senator she reportedly voted to expand LGBTQ nondiscrimination protections (WaPo via Wikipedia, no bill number); NRA-endorsed yet said she was 'open to the debate' on gun control (no specific 2A roll call) so all self_defense cells null; pro-vaccination NP who abstained on a state vaccine-mandate-protection bill (SB1449) so industry/christian-liberty conscience cells null. Same-sex-marriage cells null (RFMA passed before she was sworn in; she has declined to take a public marriage position).

2.note: Co-introduced the standalone Bipartisan IVF for Military Families Act with Democrats and championed the IVF-coverage provision through House Armed Services and the House floor — an IVF-coverage mandate (which entails embryo creation/discard), contrary to the embryo-personhood position this cell measures.

3.note: Voted FOR H.R.485 Protecting Health Care for All Patients Act, banning Quality-Adjusted-Life-Year (QALY) metrics that ration care for the disabled/elderly — opposes quality-of-life rationing.

2.note: Voted FOR the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (bar transgender athletes from girls'/women's sports) and as a VA state senator introduced a comparable bill; public statements affirm biological sex in sports.

1.note: Voted FOR H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights Act, which establishes parental rights to review curriculum/records and to be notified of school actions affecting a minor (incl. gender-identity changes) — supports parental notification/consent.
Voted FOR the SAVE Act (H.R.8281) requiring documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote — supports citizenship verification.

0.note: Voted FOR H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (resumes border-wall construction, +22,000 Border Patrol agents) — supports a completed physical barrier.

1.note: Named GOP cosponsor of the DIGNIDAD/Dignity Act (H.R.4393) creating a 7-year renewable 'Dignity Status' / pathway to legal status for ~12M undocumented immigrants present before 12/31/2020 — directly contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

3.note: H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (which she voted FOR) mandates nationwide E-Verify for employment eligibility.

1.note: Voted FOR the April 2024 Ukraine/Israel/Taiwan security supplementals and publicly criticized GOP 'isolationist' hardliners ('I refuse to sit by as the world implodes') — contrary to withdrawal from foreign entanglements; hawkish defense posture.

profile.background: Former U.S. Representative, VA-02 (2019-2023, 116th-117th Congress); retired U.S. Navy commander (20 years, surface warfare). Vice Chair of House Armed Services; member of the Jan 6 Select Committee. Lost 2022 to Jen Kiggans (R). Announced a 2026 comeback bid Nov 12 2025; certified frontrunner in the Aug 4 2026 VA-02 Democratic primary (endorsed by Sens. Warner & Kaine, Gov. Spanberger, VoteVets).

elaine-luria.notes_set: Former U.S. Rep VA-02 (2019-2023); retired Navy commander; Jan 6 Committee; 2026 comeback candidate (Aug 4 D primary frontrunner). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Scored on her REAL congressional roll-call record. Down-the-line progressive on the social/cultural and gun/election dimensions (Heritage Action lifetime 3%; NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All 100%; Giffords-endorsed): voted YES to codify abortion (Women's Health Protection Act) → sanctity FALSE; voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act → biblical_marriage FALSE; voted YES on the Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (H.R.1808) and is Giffords-endorsed → self_defense FALSE; championed the For the People Act (H.R.1), which overrides state photo-ID requirements and mandates expanded mail/early voting → election_integrity FALSE on voter-ID, single-day, and mail-in cells; voted YES on Build Back Better (~$1.75T) → economic_stewardship deficit cell FALSE. DOCUMENTED CONSERVATIVE-DIRECTION DEVIATIONS confined to national-security hawkishness, scored FALSE on the foreign_policy_restraint (non-interventionist) dimension: the ONLY Democrat to vote AGAINST repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (June 2021), and voted AGAINST the Iran War Powers Resolution (Jan 2020) — siding against constraining presidential war powers; aggressive pro-defense-spending/shipbuilding hawk. SOFT border-security lean noted (a 'security Democrat' who backed 'some physical barrier' in 2019) but left UNSCORED — it does not meet the 'completed barrier + active military presence' bar. Family/child-sovereignty, Christian-liberty, CBDC/sound-money, pharma/Big-Ag cells null — no public record located.

0.note: Voted YES on the Women's Health Protection Act (H.R.3755) to codify abortion rights nationally and holds a 100% NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All rating — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted to codify nationwide abortion access — the opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act (H.R.8404, House Roll Call 373) codifying federal recognition of same-sex marriage — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: YES on the Respect for Marriage Act — supports same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Voted YES on the Build Back Better Act (~$1.75T new spending) — supports large deficit-financed spending, contrary to balanced-budget/anti-deficit discipline.

1.note: Championed the For the People Act (H.R.1), which restricts state photo-voter-ID laws by requiring acceptance of sworn statements in lieu of ID — contrary to a photo-ID/citizenship-verification requirement.

2.note: For the People Act (H.R.1) mandates automatic/same-day registration and expanded early voting — contrary to single-day in-person voting.

3.note: For the People Act (H.R.1) mandates no-excuse mail-in voting and drop boxes — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting and drop boxes.

1.note: Voted YES on the Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (H.R.1808, House Roll Call 410) and is a Giffords-endorsed gun-control champion supporting magazine limits and red-flag laws — supports the bans this cell opposes.

0.note: Voted AGAINST the January 2020 Iran War Powers Resolution (one of ~8 Democrats), declining to require congressional authorization before military action against Iran — contrary to the Article I war-powers requirement.

1.note: The ONLY Democrat to vote AGAINST repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (June 2021) — voted to keep the standing war authorization, contrary to withdrawing from forever wars / repealing AUMFs.

profile.background: Physician (and attorney), Hampton Roads; first-time candidate. 2026 Democratic primary candidate for U.S. House VA-02, certified onto the Aug 4 2026 ballot by the Democratic Party of Virginia (May 27 2026). A progressive challenger in a field led by frontrunner Elaine Luria; no prior voting record (scored from her own campaign issues page).

nila-devanath.notes_set: Physician; first-time progressive candidate, U.S. House VA-02; CERTIFIED onto the Aug 4 2026 D primary ballot (DPVA, 2026-05-27). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Scored from her self-authored 19-point campaign issues page (no voting record). Conventional-progressive HARD positions scored FALSE: 'protect and expand' abortion access (sanctity); 'Abolish ICE' / replace with a 'humane immigration system' (border barrier and mandatory-deportation cells FALSE); 'universal background checks, safe storage laws' (self_defense FALSE). CROSS-CURRENT — earned TWO TRUEs on foreign_policy_restraint from the anti-war left (credit where earned): 'end endless wars and prioritize diplomacy' (forever-wars cell) and 'Reject AIPAC donations' (foreign-lobby-donations cell). GENUINELY NO EVIDENCE (left null, no inference): same-sex marriage/transgender, religious liberty, election integrity/voter ID, economy/Fed specifics, pharma/vaccine. SOFT and left UNSCORED: 'fully fund public schools' (no explicit school-choice/voucher position), 'support farmers and local food systems' (does not clearly state opposition to Big Ag consolidation). No conservative (cross-party) deviation found.

0.note: Campaign issues page: 'My body. My choice. Protect and expand reproductive rights and access to abortion care.' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pledges to 'protect and expand' abortion access — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Campaign issues page heading 'Abolish ICE': 'Replace ICE with a humane immigration system that respects due process and keeps families together' — opposes a militarized physical-barrier border posture.

1.note: 'Abolish ICE'/keep-families-together stance is contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.
Campaign issues page: 'Support universal background checks, safe storage laws, and community violence prevention' — supports the gun-control measures this cell opposes.
Campaign issues page: 'End endless wars and prioritize diplomacy, peace, and investments in communities at home' — supports withdrawal from forever wars.

3.note: Campaign issues page ('No AIPAC Money'): 'Reject AIPAC donations and support an independent foreign policy' — pledges to take no foreign-backed-lobby donations.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-02 (2026 D candidate — did NOT make the Aug 4 primary ballot)

profile.background: Navy veteran (Lt. Commander, ~14 years; nuclear/surface warfare); USNA economics, Oxford MBA; public-school teacher since 2022. Filed as a 2026 Democratic candidate for U.S. House VA-02 (FEC H6VA02123) but did NOT appear on the DPVA-certified Aug 4 2026 primary ballot. No prior voting record (scored from his campaign issues pages).

john-stringfellow.notes_set: CANDIDACY CURRENCY: John 'Burk' Stringfellow filed an FEC Statement of Candidacy (H6VA02123) but is NOT on the May 27 2026 DPVA-certified VA-02 Democratic primary ballot (certified field = Devanath, Bill Fleming, Luria, Mosolf) — set status:lost / candidacy_status:not_running. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Scored from his self-authored campaign issue pages (left-populist; 'I fully support Bernie Sanders' tax policies'). HARD positions scored FALSE: 'Access to abortion is not just a matter of personal choice—it's a matter of constitutional rights,' vows to restore Roe (sanctity); explicitly opposes school vouchers ('voucher systems... reveal significant flaws') (family/child-sovereignty school-choice cell FALSE). CROSS-CURRENT scored TRUE: a MAHA-adjacent Big Ag/FDA-skeptic plank — opposes factory-farm 'mass administration of hormones and antibiotics,' names glyphosate 'a known carcinogen' (industry_capture Big-Ag cell TRUE). His Fed critique is a left QE-asset-inflation critique (NOT gold/sound-money/audit) → left null. NO EVIDENCE (null): same-sex marriage/transgender, religious liberty, immigration, guns, foreign policy, voter ID, vaccines/pharma. No social-media accounts or third-party endorsements located.

0.note: Campaign 'Women's Rights' page: abortion access is 'a matter of constitutional rights and equal protection'; calls Roe's reversal 'a direct attack' and vows to restore it — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pledges to restore and protect abortion rights — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: Campaign 'Education' page explicitly opposes school vouchers ('voucher systems... reveal significant flaws,' subsidizing families already in private school) — contrary to universal school choice.

2.note: Campaign 'Chronic Disease Epidemic' page opposes factory-farm consolidation and 'mass administration of hormones and antibiotics' and names glyphosate 'a known carcinogen' — aligns with opposing Big Ag consolidation.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-02 (2026 D candidate — did NOT make the Aug 4 primary ballot)

profile.background: First-time candidate; previously ran for U.S. Senate in Washington State (2020). Filed as a 2026 Democratic candidate for U.S. House VA-02 (FEC H2VA02098, ~$5.4K raised, $0 cash on hand, no functioning campaign website) but did NOT appear on the DPVA-certified Aug 4 2026 primary ballot. No prior voting record; very thin self-stated record.

nicolaus-sleister.notes_set: CANDIDACY CURRENCY: Nicolaus 'Nick' Sleister filed FEC H2VA02098 but is NOT on the May 27 2026 DPVA-certified VA-02 Democratic primary ballot (certified field = Devanath, Bill Fleming, Luria, Mosolf); resource-starved campaign (~$5.4K raised, $0 cash, broken website) — set status:lost / candidacy_status:not_running. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier — national autopilot] Record is THIN. One HARD issue-specific self-statement via the Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey: supports 'reproductive freedom' and legal protections for abortion access → sanctity FALSE. Everything else is slogan-level (LGBTQ+ equality, humane immigration, protect entitlements) → null, not scored. His 2021 'rid government of corporate and pharma money' line is dated/out-of-state campaign-finance rhetoric, not a pharma-liability/mandate position → not mapped. NO EVIDENCE on religious liberty and guns (flat blanks). No cross-party deviation. Nothing fabricated to fill gaps.

0.note: Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey: supports 'reproductive freedom' and legal protections for abortion access — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Self-states support for abortion-access protections — opposite of abortion-abolition.
